topics: 🐔 Tied thread on a chicken's foot — Rav Huna: who tied it?
🌬️ Pecking, scraping, falling, flying — full vs. half damage
🍇 Symmachos vs. tanna kamma on cutting (קרטום) vs. breaking (שיבור)
🦷 שן: how does Resh Lakish vs. R' Yochanan read 'הוּשְּׁבָה'?
🦵 Walking kicks lying down: when is keren in רה"ר exempt?

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Tooth, Foot, and Wings
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: Daf 9 plays out three connected debates. First, Rav Huna distinguishes between a thread that tied itself onto a chicken's foot and one the owner tied — the second is full damage. Then a long beraita maps every chicken motion (pecking, scraping, falling, flying, blowing) onto full or half. Then Mishnah 2:3 opens the שן sugya, and Resh Lakish vs. R' Yochanan disagree whether the Sages' answer to R' Tarphon (הוּשְׁבָה) covers the whole Mishnah or only its first clause — a dispute that decides what happens when a walking animal kicks one lying down.
overview.hebrewSource: רֵישׁ לָקִישׁ אָמַר. עַל הָרִאשׁוֹנָה הוּשְּׁבָה. רִבִּי יוֹחָנָן אָמַר. עַל כּוּלָּהּ הוּשְּׁבָה

slides.title: 💬 Rav Huna on the Tied Thread
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah said half damage for a chicken with a thread on its foot. Rav Huna asks: who tied it?

lines.speaker: M
lines.text: הָיָה דְלִיל קָשׁוּר בְּרַגְלוֹ... מְשַׁלֵּם חֲצִי נֶזֶק

lines.speaker: Rav Huna
lines.text: בְּשֶׁנִּקְשַׁר מֵאֵילָיו — only when the thread tied itself. אֲבָל אִם קְשָׁרוֹ הוּא — מְשַׁלֵּם נֶזֶק שָׁלֵם.

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: If the owner tied it, he turned a chicken into a hazard. The half-damage rule for keren-style abnormal motion no longer applies — he pays in full.

callout.label: Why this matters:
callout.text: Rav Huna is locating the half-damage rule precisely: it's for accidents of nature, not for hazards a person creates. Once the owner ties the string, the chicken becomes a moving bor in his name.

slides.title: 📊 The Beraita's Catalogue of Chicken Damage
slides.subtitle: A taxonomy of every chicken motion, with payments for each.
rows: Pecked rope, pail snapped or broke

callout.label: Pattern:
callout.text: Direct bodily contact = full. Indirect force (dust, wind) = half. Symmachos pushes back — for him, even wind is normal chicken behavior and pays in full.

slides.title: 🪣 The Falling Chicken — Why Not a בור?
slides.subtitle: R' Yose ben R' Bun fixes a tricky comparison.

nodes.content:
נָפַל עַל חֲבֵירוֹ
Upper chicken pays full, lower pays half

Objection: וְאֵינוֹ כְעוֹשֶׂה בוֹר בְּמָקוֹם אֶחָד וּמַזִּיק בְּמָקוֹם אַחֵר?
Isn't this like a pit dug here that damages there — which is exempt?

R' Yose b. R' Bun: מִכֵּיוָן שֶׁאֵין דַּרְכּוֹ לִיפּוֹל אֶלָּא בְאוֹתוֹ הַבּוֹר כְּמִי שֶׁהוּא בוֹרוֹ
Since it only naturally falls into that pit, the pit becomes 'his'

callout.label: The move:
callout.text: R' Yose collapses the bor problem by re-attributing the pit. The lower chicken did not dig; the upper chicken's natural fall makes the destination effectively his own bor — so liability holds.

slides.title: 💬 Resh Lakish vs. R' Yochanan on the Mishnah of שן
slides.subtitle: How much of Mishnah 2:3 did the Sages 'send back' (הוּשְׁבָה)?

lines.speaker: RL
lines.text: עַל הָרִאשׁוֹנָה הוּשְּׁבָה — only the first clause was answered. → A walking animal that kicks one lying down: exempt.

lines.speaker: R' Yo
lines.text: עַל כּוּלָּהּ הוּשְּׁבָה — the whole Mishnah was answered. → Same case: liable.

lines.speaker: R' Immi
lines.text: RL only exempts where a walking animal kicks a lying one. But lying-kicks-walking, or walking-kicks-walking — even RL holds liable.

lines.speaker: R' Hoshaia
lines.text: כּוּלָּהּ פָּטוּר — all such cases are exempt. אֵין קֶרֶן בִּרְשׁוּת הָרַבִּים? — there's no keren liability in רה"ר at all in this scenario!

callout.label: Yerushalmi style:
callout.text: A whole halakhic landscape is unfolded from one word, הוּשְׁבָה. The reach of the Sages' reply decides who pays in every walking/lying combination.

slides.title: 🦷 שן vs. קרן: The Symmetry the Yerushalmi Builds
slides.subtitle: Rav and Ilfai use a parallel between leniency for tooth and stringency for horn.

steps.content: 1️⃣ שן (tooth): if the animal stood still and ate from a heap — חַיֶיבֶת (Rav).
2️⃣ Leniency for שן: a walking animal eating from a standing heap — exempt.
3️⃣ קרן (horn): mirror image — a walking animal that kicks a standing one is liable.
4️⃣ Ilfai pushes further: even walking-kicks-walking is liable — keren is treated stringently across the board.
5️⃣ Net rule: לעיכוב — שן is more forgiving by motion-state; קרן is harsher by motion-state.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: The Yerushalmi builds the שן/קרן contrast as a structural symmetry: where one is lenient, the other is stringent — and motion (walking, lying, standing) is the diagnostic feature for both.

summary.principle: Direct vs. indirect, normal vs. unusual, walking vs. lying down — daf 9 catalogues every variable that decides full or half damage for tooth, foot, and the in-between motions of chickens.

flowSteps.content: Mishnah: chickens are מוּעָדִין לְהַלֵּךְ כְּדַרְכָּן וּלְשַׁבֵּר
Rav Huna: did the thread tie itself, or did the owner tie it?

🎓 Key Principle:
Liability tracks intent, directness, and the motion-state of both animals. שן and קרן form a mirror: where one is lenient, the other is stringent.

quiz.q: According to Rav Huna, when does the chicken-with-thread case pay full damages instead of half?
o: When the chicken is muad
When the thread tied itself onto the foot
When the owner himself tied the thread
Never — it is always half
quiz.e: Rav Huna: בְּשֶׁנִּקְשַׁר מֵאֵילָיו half damage; אֲבָל אִם קְשָׁרוֹ הוּא מְשַׁלֵּם נֶזֶק שָׁלֵם.

quiz.q: If a chicken's wing-wind (רוּחַ שֶׁבֵּין כְּנָפָיו) breaks something, what does the tanna kamma say?
o: Full damages
Half damages
Exempt
Only the value of benefit
quiz.e: Tanna kamma: half damages because it is indirect. Symmachos disagrees and says full.

quiz.q: How does R' Yose b. R' Bun answer the objection about a chicken falling on another (the bor-here-damages-there problem)?
o: He concedes and exempts the upper chicken
He says אֵין קֶרֶן בִּרְשׁוּת הָרַבִּים
מִכֵּיוָן שֶׁאֵין דַּרְכּוֹ לִיפּוֹל אֶלָּא בְאוֹתוֹ הַבּוֹר כְּמִי שֶׁהוּא בוֹרוֹ
He says it depends on whether it was muad
quiz.e: Because the chicken only falls naturally into that one place, the destination is treated as if it were the chicken's own pit.

quiz.q: What is the dispute between Resh Lakish and R' Yochanan on Mishnah 2:3?
o: Whether a chicken's wind is full or half damage
Whether the Sages' answer (הוּשְׁבָה) covers only the first clause or all of it
Whether שן is exempt in רה"ר
Whether dogs and goats are muad
quiz.e: Resh Lakish: עַל הָרִאשׁוֹנָה הוּשְּׁבָה. R' Yochanan: עַל כּוּלָּהּ הוּשְּׁבָה. Their dispute determines whether walking-kicks-lying is exempt or liable.

quiz.q: According to R' Immi, what is Resh Lakish's actual exempt case?
o: Lying animal kicking a walking one
Walking animal kicking a walking one
Walking animal kicking one that is lying down (בָּעֲטָה מְהַלֶּכֶת בָּרְבוּצָה)
All three are exempt
quiz.e: R' Immi narrows Resh Lakish: only walking-kicks-lying is exempt. The other combinations remain liable.
